vocabulary.example: My colleague helped me finish the project.

vocabulary.meaning: The latest time by which something must be done
Moving to a higher position at work
vocabulary.example: She got a promotion last month.

vocabulary.meaning: To discuss to reach an agreement
vocabulary.exampleBn: সে আরও ভালো বেতনের জন্য আলোচনা করল।

phrases.example: Don't worry, I'm working on it.

phrases.phrase: Let me get back to you
phrases.usage: When you need time before answering
phrases.example: That's a great question. Let me get back to you.

phrases.bangla: আমরা কি সময় পরিবর্তন করতে পারি?
phrases.usage: When you need to change an appointment
phrases.example: I have a conflict. Could we reschedule our meeting?

phrases.usage: Responding to comments about your work
phrases.example: Thank you. I appreciate your feedback on this report.

sentences.en: I have been working here for three years.
sentences.bn: আমি এখানে তিন বছর ধরে কাজ করছি।

sentences.en: Could you please send me the report by Friday?

sentences.en: I would like to apply for the manager position.
sentences.bn: আমি ম্যানেজার পদের জন্য আবেদন করতে চাই।

sentences.en: We need to meet the deadline or we'll lose the client.
sentences.bn: আমাদের সময়সীমা পূরণ করতে হবে নয়তো আমরা ক্লায়েন্ট হারাব।

quiz.explanation: A colleague is someone who works in the same organization as you.

quiz.q: 'Let me get back to you' means:
opts: I will call you back later
I will give you information later
quiz.explanation: This phrase means you'll provide an answer or update at a later time.
A deadline is the final date or time by which something must be completed.

quiz.q: How do you ask to change a meeting time?
quiz.explanation: 'Could we reschedule?' is the polite way to request a time change.

story.title: The Big Presentation
story.body:
Nadia had an important presentation at work. The deadline was Monday morning.

On Friday, her colleague Rahim asked, "How's the report going, Nadia?"

"I'm working on it," she said, "but I need more data."

"I can help with that," said Rahim. "Let's work together this weekend."

They met on Saturday and finished the report. Nadia practiced her presentation many times.

On Monday, she spoke confidently. Her manager was impressed.

After the meeting, the manager said, "Excellent work, Nadia. I appreciate your dedication. We're considering you for a promotion."

Nadia smiled. "Thank you! I appreciate your feedback. I've really been working hard."

She walked out of the office happier than ever.

comprehension.q: What good news did Nadia receive?

listening.title: Office Conversation
listening.script:
Manager: "John, could you send me the quarterly report by Friday?"

John: "Of course. I'm working on it right now."

Manager: "Great. How are things going with the new client?"

John: "We're still negotiating the contract. They want a better price."

Manager: "That's fine. Let me know how it goes. Could we reschedule our 3 PM meeting? I have a conflict."

John: "Sure! Would 4 PM work?"

Manager: "Perfect. Also, I wanted to say — I appreciate your feedback on the new system. It was very helpful."

John: "Thank you. I just want to make sure we meet all our deadlines."

Manager: "That's exactly why we're thinking about your promotion. Keep up the good work!"

comprehension.q: What document does the manager need?
Why does the manager want to reschedule?

speaking.topic: Talk About Your Work or Studies
speaking.prompt: Describe your work or studies. What do you do? What challenges do you face? Use work-related vocabulary.
speaking.example: I am a student at university. My deadline for the final project is next month. I work with my colleagues — classmates — every day. I hope to get a promotion in my career someday.
tips: Use present tense for current situations
Use 'I have been...' for ongoing experiences
